titleStmt.title: SII 9.65: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 65. (A.R. No. 53 of 1904.) ON A SLAB SET UP AT THE ENTRANCE TO THE VILLAGE KUDATINI, BELLARY TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: The record is dated Ňöaka 870, Kńęlaka, ŇörńĀvaŠĻáa, Ňõuddha pa√Īchamńę, ńÄdityavńĀra, corresponding to A.D. 947 July 25 Sunday; the year was Kńęlaka by the Northern cycle. It refers itself to the reign of the RńĀshŠĻ≠rakŇęŠĻ≠a king ChalakenallńĀta AkńĀŠł∑avarshadńďva (KŠĻõishŠĻáa III) and records that a gift of 62 mattars of wet land, free of tax, made by the MahńĀjanas of KuŠĻüuŠĻÖgŇćŠłću to the temple of Dńďkamma and which had lapsed was regranted by the king at the instance of RudrarńĀŇõi-BhaŠĻ≠ńĀra. LŇćkayya-GńĀvuŠĻáŠłća, a pious BrńĀhman in charge of the locality KńĀrtikńďya-tapŇćvana, is said to have given an oil mill to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
